Tax Modeling Toolkit Section 1 Introduction 1.1 Main purpose 1.2 Example data

Tax Modeling Toolkit World Bank Tax Team Section 1 Introduction This website presents an example of an HTML documentation page created with R. The website can be used to document a tax modeling toolkit covering: VAT PIT CIT Customs duties Excise duties The website is static HTML, but it can include R tables, R charts, formulas, and links to Shiny applications. 1.1 Main purpose The purpose of the toolkit is to provide a structured framework for: preparing tax data; defining baseline and reform scenarios; estimating revenue impacts; presenting simulation results; documenting assumptions and methodology. 1.2 Example data head(tax_results) ## tax_type year revenue_baseline revenue_simulation ## 1 VAT 2024 1000 1080 ## 2 VAT 2025 1100 1190 ## 3 PIT 2024 700 740 ## 4 PIT 2025 760 820 ## 5 CIT 2024 500 530 ## 6 CIT 2025 540 600
Section 2 Overview of the Modeling Framework 2.1 Main workflow 2.2 Example summary table

Section 2 Overview of the Modeling Framework The modeling framework is organized around a baseline scenario and one or more reform scenarios. The general workflow is: Import raw data. Clean and structure the data. Define tax policy parameters. Estimate baseline liability. Simulate reform scenarios. Compare baseline and reform results. Present revenue and distributional effects. 2.1 Main workflow workflow_chart 2.2 Example summary table summary_table ## # A tibble: 3 × 5 ## tax_type Revenue_Baseline Revenue_Simulation Revenue_Change Revenue_Change_Percent ## <chr> <int> <int> <int> <dbl> ## 1 CIT 1040 1130 90 8.65 ## 2 PIT 1460 1560 100 6.85 ## 3 VAT 2100 2270 170 8.10
Section 3 VAT Module 3.1 Main data sources 3.2 Example VAT results 3.3 VAT revenue chart 3.4 Methodological note

Section 3 VAT Module The VAT module estimates potential VAT revenue under the current policy structure and under alternative reform scenarios. 3.1 Main data sources The VAT module may use: Supply and Use Tables; Input-Output Tables; household final consumption; government final consumption; NPISH final consumption; intermediate consumption; gross fixed capital formation; VAT revenue data; VAT policy rates and exemptions. 3.2 Example VAT results vat_table ## # A tibble: 1 × 5 ## tax_type Revenue_Baseline Revenue_Simulation Revenue_Change Revenue_Change_Percent ## <chr> <int> <int> <int> <dbl> ## 1 VAT 2100 2270 170 8.10 3.3 VAT revenue chart vat_chart 3.4 Methodological note The VAT revenue impact can be calculated as: vat_revenue_change <- sum(tax_results$revenue_simulation - tax_results$revenue_baseline) vat_revenue_change ## [1] 360
Section 4 PIT Module 4.1 Main policy parameters 4.2 Example PIT results 4.3 PIT revenue chart 4.4 Example interpretation

Section 4 PIT Module The Personal Income Tax module estimates tax liability at taxpayer level or household level. 4.1 Main policy parameters The PIT module can include: tax rates; tax brackets; personal allowances; deductions; exemptions; special regimes; withholding tax rates. 4.2 Example PIT results pit_table ## # A tibble: 1 × 5 ## tax_type Revenue_Baseline Revenue_Simulation Revenue_Change Revenue_Change_Percent ## <chr> <int> <int> <int> <dbl> ## 1 PIT 1460 1560 100 6.85 4.3 PIT revenue chart pit_chart 4.4 Example interpretation The PIT simulation compares revenue under the baseline system and the reform scenario. pit_change <- pit_table$Revenue_Simulation - pit_table$Revenue_Baseline pit_change ## [1] 100
Section 5 User Guide 5.1 Step 1: Prepare input data 5.2 Step 2: Run the scripts 5.3 Step 3: Open the HTML website 5.4 Step 4: Update the results 5.5 Optional: link to a Shiny app

Section 5 User Guide This section explains how users can work with the model. 5.1 Step 1: Prepare input data All input data should be saved in the data/ folder. Required variables in the example file are: tax_type year revenue_baseline revenue_simulation 5.2 Step 2: Run the scripts Run the following script: source("build_site.R") 5.3 Step 3: Open the HTML website After rendering, open: _book/index.html 5.4 Step 4: Update the results To update the website, replace the input file: data/tax_model_results.csv Then run again: bookdown::render_book("index.Rmd") 5.5 Optional: link to a Shiny app You can also include a link to an interactive Shiny app: [Open VAT Simulation App](https://your-account.shinyapps.io/vat_app/)
